original_hypothesis.title: DefaultTargetDispenserL2 processDataMaintenance Allows Owner to Bypass Staking Verification
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The processDataMaintenance function allows the owner to call _processData with arbitrary data, which routes through the same verification pipeline as bridge messages. However, the owner can craft data targeting any address with any amount. While _processData does check via stakingFactory, the function effectively gives the owner ability to queue deposits to arbitrary targets.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: processDataMaintenance exists for disaster recovery (bridge failures). However, it bypasses bridge authentication entirely - the owner supplies both targets and amounts directly. If the owner is compromised or malicious, they can direct withheld/queued OLAS to any target, even ones that fail verification (which would be withheld, not deposited). The concern is that valid-appearing targets receive unearned deposits.

proof_of_signal.pattern: processDataMaintenance calls _processData with owner-supplied data, bypassing bridge message authentication; targets still verified by factory but amounts are unconstrained

preconditions: L2 target dispenser owner is compromised or acts maliciously
L2 dispenser has OLAS balance (from previous bridge transfers or withheld amounts)
Owner crafts data to direct OLAS to specific staking targets

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No bridge authentication; owner provides (targets, amounts) directly
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Compromised owner directs all OLAS balance to attacker-controlled staking contracts
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Full OLAS balance of L2 dispenser extracted via attacker-controlled staking targets

economic_questions: How much OLAS typically sits in L2 dispensers?
Does stakingFactory verification adequately limit the damage?
Is the owner a timelock/multisig or a single EOA?
validation_requirements: Verify that processDataMaintenance data is processed identically to bridge messages
Confirm factory verification still applies to maintenance-supplied targets
Check if amount limits are enforced on maintenance data

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Full OLAS balance of L2 dispenser
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Very high if balance is significant

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When L2 dispenser has maximum OLAS balance
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Loss of all pending staking incentives for the chain

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All staking targets on the affected L2 chain

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Typical L2 dispenser OLAS balance, owner type (multisig/timelock)
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify factory verification limits damage in maintenance path

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
